step_by_step_thinking:
The odontoblasts mentioned here refer to the cells that form dentin, which is derived from mesoderm. Intestinal epithelium (option A), goblet cells (which secrete mucus and are found within intestinal or respiratory epithelia - option C), and hepatocytes (liver cells; option D) all originate from the endoderm during embryonic development.

My Answer: B To clarify further for those reviewing this case: 
Odontoblasts develop as part of dental tissue formation through the ectomesenchyme pathway originating primarily from the neural crest rather than directly from the endoderm layer, making them distinct among these choices based on their developmental origin. 

Final
